name: Humphrey Bogart
abstract: Humphrey DeForest Bogart ( BOH-gart; December 25, 1899 – January 14, 1957), nicknamed Bogie, was an American actor. His performances in classic Hollywood cinema made him an American cultural icon.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Pittsburgh Courier Poll Winners Jazz Concert

about_quotes.speaker: Big Chielf Moore
about_quotes.evidence: They called him Bogie because he wore a hat -- he wore it like Humphrey Bogart would, you know.

about_quotes.speaker: Jack Sheldon
about_quotes.evidence: He did Tony Bennett and he did him so good, and he did Humphrey Bogart and everything.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jack Sheldon interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Woody Herman
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee confirms Humphrey Bogart owned the house and lived there with Lauren Bacall, but no personal interaction or relationship between Woody Herman and Humphrey Bogart is described.

about_quotes.speaker: Teddy Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: Humphrey Bogart had that little club, and they got me to play the piano, because the original pianist in the movie, the actor, Dooley Wilson, had passed away

about_quotes.speaker: Ran Blake
about_quotes.evidence: I might switch to Humphrey Bogart, Ethel Barrymore, and Dorothy Dandridge. So these memories come right back to me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ran Blake interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 16, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Clark Terry
about_quotes.evidence: we were in the Strand Theater with... Humphrey Bogart and Count Basie and Billie Holiday.
about_quotes.doc_title: Clark Terry interviewed by Joe Williams and Michael Woods, Clinton, New York, May 19, 1995
